name: Mead Coated Board
description_short: Federal credit union established in 1967 serving manufacturing sector employees in Pittsview, Alabama with savings accounts, auto loans, and personal banking services.
description_long: Mead Coated Board Federal Credit Union was established in 1967 and is headquartered in Pittsview, Alabama. Originally created to serve employees of the coated board manufacturing industry, the institution has maintained its focus on providing financial services to diverse manufacturing sectors for over five decades. The credit union offers a range of personal banking products including regular savings accounts, share draft accounts, money market accounts, and share certificates (CDs). Lending products include new and used auto loans, personal loans, mortgages with adjustable rate options, and unsecured lines of credit. Members also have access to IRA/KEOGH accounts, online banking, mobile apps for iOS and Android, and online bill pay services. As a not-for-profit federal credit union, Mead Coated Board distinguishes itself through competitive rates that typically exceed those of for-profit banks, including 3.99% APR on new vehicle loans and 7.00% on used vehicles. The institution maintains a small, focused operation with 1 branch, approximately 2 employees, and serves over 1,500 members while stewarding $46.8 million in assets. Their membership is specifically tailored to manufacturing sector employees, reflecting their origin and ongoing mission. The credit union's limitations are significant: it operates only one physical branch location with no ATM network, making accessibility challenging for members outside Pittsview. The current lobby is closed, and online/mobile services are the primary access channels. With such limited physical infrastructure and a specialized membership base tied to manufacturing employment, this institution serves a narrow geographic and occupational market rather than the general public.
services: Mortgage loans with adjustable rate options
pros: Competitive rates on auto loans (3.99% new, 7.00% used) compared to traditional banks
Not-for-profit structure typically results in lower fees and better member value
Serves 1,500+ loyal members with $46.8 million in assets, demonstrating stability
Offers comprehensive loan products including mortgages, auto loans, and personal loans
Mobile banking app available for both iOS and Android devices
Online banking and bill pay services for convenient account management
Membership tailored to specific manufacturing sectors with industry-focused solutions
cons: Only 1 branch location in Pittsview, Alabama with no ATM network, limiting accessibility
Lobby currently closed, forcing members to rely exclusively on online/mobile banking
Membership restricted to manufacturing sector employees, not open to general public
Very small institution with only 2 employees, potentially limiting service capacity
No reviews available on the website, making member satisfaction difficult to assess
best_for: Manufacturing sector employees in or near Pittsview, Alabama seeking personalized service
Members comfortable with online/mobile-first banking who don't require physical branch visits
Borrowers seeking competitive auto loan rates through a credit union structure
Long-term members of the Mead Coated Board manufacturing community
diagnosis: Mead Coated Board FCU is best for current and retired manufacturing sector employees in Pittsview, Alabama seeking competitive loan rates and basic banking services through a not-for-profit credit union. The primary caveat is that membership is restricted to specific manufacturing sectors and the institution operates solely through online/mobile channels and one closed lobby, making it inaccessible to those requiring in-person banking or living outside the immediate area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
